title: A resolution dissenting from the United States delegation's February 24, 2025, vote at the United Nations General Assembly.
plain_summary: This resolution says the Senate disagrees with how America voted at the United Nations on February 24, 2025. On that day, America voted against helping Ukraine make peace and instead voted the same way as Russia. The Senate thinks this was wrong because America should support Ukraine and work with other democratic countries. The resolution asks America to go back to supporting Ukraine and working with its democratic allies at the United Nations instead of siding with Russia.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations. (text: CR S1401-1402)

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This is a resolution that disagrees with how the United States voted at the United Nations General Assembly on February twenty fourth, twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Reasons for This Resolution
sections.text: The United States works better when it cooperates with friendly countries because this helps protect American security. On February twenty fourth, twenty twenty five, the American representatives at the United Nations voted against a resolution written by Ukraine. This Ukrainian resolution was called Advancing a comprehensive, just and lasting peace in Ukraine. When America voted against this resolution, it went against Ukraine and other democratic countries that are America's friends. Instead, America's vote put it on the same side as Russia, Belarus, North Korea, and other countries ruled by dictators. This was the first time since twenty fourteen that America voted the same way as Russia on any issue about Ukraine at the United Nations. This vote changed ten years of American policy that both political parties supported about Ukraine. It also broke eighty years of America voting with other democratic countries at the United Nations. At the United Nations Security Council, America also led a different resolution that failed to clearly say Russia was the country that invaded Ukraine. This resolution did not get support from all five European countries on the Security Council.

sections.title: What the Senate Decides
sections.text: The Senate condemns America's vote against Ukraine's resolution at the United Nations General Assembly on February twenty fourth, twenty twenty five. The Senate strongly disapproves that American representatives refused to continue calling Russia an aggressor country or to demand that Russia completely remove all its military forces from Ukrainian territory within Ukraine's official borders. The Senate remembers that the main goals of the United Nations Charter are to maintain peace and security and to stop acts of aggression or other actions that break the peace. The Senate urges America to work closely with Ukraine and European allies on future efforts at the United Nations that relate to Ukraine. The Senate reaffirms its support for Ukraine's sovereignty and territorial integrity within its internationally recognized borders.
